title: Okahandja

infobox.value: is a city[[3]](#cite_note-ALANlist-3) of 45,159 inhabitants in [Otjozondjupa Region](/wiki/Otjozondjupa "Otjozondjupa"), central [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"), and the district capital of the [Okahandja electoral constituency](/wiki/Okahandja_Constituency "Okahandja Constituency"). It is known as the *Garden Town of Namibia*. It is located 70 km north of [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek") on the [B1 road](/wiki/B1_road_(Namibia) "B1 road (Namibia)"). It was founded around 1800, by two local groups, the [Herero](/wiki/Herero_people "Herero people") and the [Nama](/wiki/Nama_people "Nama people").[[4]](#cite_note-OT-4)

**Okahandja** is a city[[3]](#cite_note-ALANlist-3) of 45,159 inhabitants in [Otjozondjupa Region](/wiki/Otjozondjupa "Otjozondjupa"), central [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"), and the district capital of the [Okahandja electoral constituency](/wiki/Okahandja_Constituency "Okahandja Constituency"). It is known as the *Garden Town of Namibia*. It is located 70 km north of [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek") on the [B1 road](/wiki/B1_road_(Namibia) "B1 road (Namibia)"). It was founded around 1800, by two local groups, the [Herero](/wiki/Herero_people "Herero people") and the [Nama](/wiki/Nama_people "Nama people").[[4]](#cite_note-OT-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In the [Herero language](/wiki/Herero_language "Herero language"), Okahandja means *the place where two rivers converge*, with the two rivers being the Okakango and the Okamita.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

A [German](/wiki/Germans "Germans") pastor, [Heinrich Schmelen](/wiki/Heinrich_Schmelen "Heinrich Schmelen"), became the first [European](/wiki/European_ethnic_groups "European ethnic groups") to visit the town in 1827.[[6]](#cite_note-Dierks-6) In 1844, two missionaries were permanently assigned to the town, [Heinrich Kleinschmidt](/wiki/Heinrich_Kleinschmidt "Heinrich Kleinschmidt") and [Hugo Hahn](/wiki/Hugo_Hahn "Hugo Hahn"). A church dates from this period. A military post was established at the initiative of [Theodor Leutwein](/wiki/Theodor_Leutwein "Theodor Leutwein") in 1894, and it is this date that is officially recognized as the town's founding.[[7]](#cite_note-OH-7)

A number of important historic Namibian people are buried in Okahandja, among them [Maharero](/wiki/Maharero "Maharero"), [Jan Jonker Afrikaner](/wiki/Jan_Jonker_Afrikaner "Jan Jonker Afrikaner"), [Hosea Kutako](/wiki/Hosea_Kutako "Hosea Kutako") and [Clemens Kapuuo](/wiki/Clemens_Kapuuo "Clemens Kapuuo").

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy")]

The population of Okahandja is growing rapidly. It stood at just over 14,000 as measured by the 2001 Population and Housing Census, and is estimated to have surpassed 24,000 in 2012.[[8]](#cite_note-2001Census-8)[[9]](#cite_note-debt-9)

[Von Bach Dam](/wiki/Von_Bach_Dam "Von Bach Dam") is situated outside of Okahandja. It provides the majority of [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek")'s water. An open-air curio market attracts tourists, and the town serves as the administrative centre for the [Herero people](/wiki/Herero_people "Herero people").

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Transport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cc/Bahnhof_Okahandja_1903.jpg/250px-Bahnhof_Okahandja_1903.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bahnhof_Okahandja_1903.jpg)

[Okahandja Railway Station](/wiki/Okahandja_Railway_Station "Okahandja Railway Station"), 1902

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2a/Maharero_monument_Okahandja.jpg/250px-Maharero_monument_Okahandja.jpg)](/wiki/File:Maharero_monument_Okahandja.jpg)

Monument to Herero Chiefs: Gravestone of [Tjamuaha](/wiki/Tjamuaha "Tjamuaha"), [Maharero](/wiki/Maharero "Maharero"), and [Samuel Maharero](/wiki/Samuel_Maharero "Samuel Maharero")

[Okahandja Railway Station](/wiki/Okahandja_Railway_Station "Okahandja Railway Station"), situated on the [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek")—[Swakopmund](/wiki/Swakopmund "Swakopmund") line, was built in 1902 during [Imperial Germany's](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire") colonial rule of [German South West Africa](/wiki/German_South_West_Africa "German South West Africa"). Today it belongs to the [Trans-Namib](/wiki/Trans-Namib "Trans-Namib") railway network.[[10]](#cite_note-KDRail-10)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education")]

In the 1870s [Rhenish missionaries](/wiki/Rhenish_Missionary_Society "Rhenish Missionary Society") established the first school. The [Augustineum School](/wiki/Augustineum,_Namibia "Augustineum, Namibia") was reopened at Okahandja on 9 November 1905; it was later moved to [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek").
Today[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit) Okahandja has six primary schools and two high schools. Namwater Vocational, situated outside the main town, is the only institute of higher education in Okahandja.

The National Institute for Educational Development (NIED) is today situated in Okahandja. NIED was created after independence as the institute from where a new national system of education was created, replacing the previous racist system of apartheid.

Previously the German school Regierungsschule Okahandja was in the city.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### List of schools in Okahandja

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: List of schools in Okahandja")]

1. Okahandja Secondary School[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Politics")]

Okahandja is governed by a municipal council that has seven seats.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In the [2010 local authority election](/wiki/Namibian_local_and_regional_elections,_2010 "Namibian local and regional elections, 2010") in Okahandja, [SWAPO](/wiki/SWAPO "SWAPO") won with approximately 62% of the vote. Of the five other parties seeking votes in the election, [Rally for Democracy and Progress](/wiki/Rally_for_Democracy_and_Progress_(Namibia) "Rally for Democracy and Progress (Namibia)") (RDP) received approximately 13% of the vote, followed by the [United Democratic Front](/wiki/United_Democratic_Front_(Namibia) "United Democratic Front (Namibia)") (UDF, 8%), the [United People's Movement](/wiki/United_People%27s_Movement_(Namibia) "United People's Movement (Namibia)") (UPM, 7%), [National Unity Democratic Organization](/wiki/National_Unity_Democratic_Organization "National Unity Democratic Organization") (NUDO, 6%) and [Democratic Turnhalle Alliance](/wiki/Democratic_Turnhalle_Alliance "Democratic Turnhalle Alliance") (DTA, 2%).[[14]](#cite_note-14) The [2015 local authority election](/wiki/Namibian_local_and_regional_elections,_2015 "Namibian local and regional elections, 2015") was again won by SWAPO which gained five seats and 2,572 votes. One seat each went to the DTA and the UDF with 236 and 213 votes, respectively.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

SWAPO also won the [2020 local authority election](/wiki/2020_Namibian_local_and_regional_elections "2020 Namibian local and regional elections") but lost its absolute majority in the municipal council. It obtained 1,865 votes and gained three seats. One seat each went to the [Independent Patriots for Change](/wiki/Independent_Patriots_for_Change "Independent Patriots for Change") (IPC, an opposition party formed in August 2020, 654 votes), the local [Okahandja Rate Payers' Association](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja_Rate_Payers%27_Association&action=edit&redlink=1 "Okahandja Rate Payers' Association (page does not exist)") (ORPA, 574 votes), the [Landless People's Movement](/wiki/Landless_People%27s_Movement_(Namibia) "Landless People's Movement (Namibia)") (LPM, an opposition party formed in 2016, 377 votes), and the UDF (290 votes).[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Okahandja&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sport")]

Okahandja offers the following sports codes; Soccer, Netball, Basketball, Tennis, Horse Raising, Dancing, Running, Gym, Spin & Drifting, Radio Helicopters...

[Okahandja United FC](/wiki/Okahandja_United_FC "Okahandja United FC") competes in the [Namibia Premier Football League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_Football_League "Namibia Premier Football League") as of the 2023.[[17]](#cite_note-Make_it_difficult-17) The oldest [soccer club](/wiki/Soccer_club "Soccer club") in town is known as Spoilers Sports Club that was founded in 1963 and managed over the years by "Ou Boss" Reinhardt Maletzky. In the 1970s, Okahandja Soccer Club had a competitive football team that won several competitions.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In addition, local club [Liverpool Okahandja](/wiki/Liverpool_Okahandja "Liverpool Okahandja") were [NFA-Cup](/wiki/NFA-Cup "NFA-Cup") winners in 1992 and [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League") champions in 2002. Other local teams were Magic Tigers, Battle Boys, Teenagers, Golden Arrows.
